seoTitle: St Georges vs St Johns — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between St Georges and St Johns. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: St Georges vs St Johns: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in St Georges and St Johns.
heading: St Georges vs St Johns — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between St Georges, South Australia and St Johns, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: St Johns has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to St Georges (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is St Georges or St Johns safer from flooding?
faq.answer: St Georges has a flood risk score of 50/100, while St Johns scores 51/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: St Georges scores 27/100 for bushfire risk. St Johns scores 28/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in St Georges or St Johns?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. St Johns has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.5% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
